t_ch: （给诊所寄送物资的好心人，看来就是科鲁泽……）
t_en: (Looks like Kluzer is the kind-hearted donor after all, who's been sending supplies to the clinic all this time...)

t_ch: （他本不该落得这个凄惨的下场…得把这件事告诉娜塔莎。）
t_en: (He shouldn't have come to this miserable end... I have to tell Natasha about this.)

t_ch: 那几个孩子，不会是趁着场面混乱，跑到什么地方冒险去了吧？
t_en: Those kids better not have taken advantage of the chaos to go on an expedition.

t_ch: 虎克之前一直吵着闹着要去铆钉镇探险…这可不妙。
t_en: Hook does always talk about wanting to go on a Rivet Town adventure... this could be bad.

t_ch: …四十八…四十九…五十！
t_en: <unbreak>48</unbreak>... <unbreak>49</unbreak>... <unbreak>50</unbreak>!

t_ch: ——藏好了吗？嘿嘿，那我要来咯！
t_en: Ready or not, here I come!

t_ch: …是尤利安？快，上去看看！
t_en: ...Julian? Quick, let's go see!

t_ch: 可算找到你们了！居然跑到这种地方玩捉迷藏，太危险了！
t_en: Found you! You ran all the way out here just to play hide-and-seek? It's too dangerous!

t_ch: 三、三月大姐？！你、你们不是回上层区了吗？
t_en: M—Miss March!? D—Didn't you go back to the surface...?

t_ch: 不许叫我「大姐」！虎克她们人呢？赶紧把大家叫出来吧。
t_en: Since when did I become "Miss" March? And where's Hook and the others? Come on, call them over.

t_ch: 唔…游戏才刚开始，就这么结束未免太扫兴了……
t_en: Aww... but we just started playing. Can't we finish this round first...?

t_ch: 那我们就再来抓一次人吧！
t_en: Say no more — this is gonna be easy!

t_ch: 看来你们之前已经玩过类似的游戏了……
t_en: Seasoned pros, I see...

t_ch: 才、才不是我想玩啊，都是被这群小孩子逼的！
t_en: Hey, it wasn't like I wanted to play! The kids forced us to...

t_ch: 拿棒棒糖钓小孩子出来吧。
t_en: If we had lollipops, they'd give themselves up in no time.

t_ch: 好主意耶！小孩子已经就位，只差棒棒糖了！
t_en: Great idea! Shame we don't have any.

t_ch: 去把剩下的孩子找出来吧。
t_en: Well, we better go and find them.

t_ch: 唔，本来我想先荡会儿秋千再…那行吧，找人的活就交给你们这些大人吧！
t_en: ...A—Actually, I sorta wanna go play on the swings for a while... I'll leave the seeking to you guys!

t_ch: 欸嘿，抓到你啦！
t_en: Aha! Gotcha!

t_ch: …咦，怎么是三个大人？尤利安上哪去了？
t_en: ...Huh? Grown-ups? Where's Julian?

t_ch: 呼…真扫兴，我还以为他会趁这个机会对我说些什么呢……
t_en: Hmph, so he is a scaredy-cat. I knew he'd get someone else to talk to me...

t_ch: 尤利安，吾辈楷模…
t_en: Well, that explains "sorta wanna go play on the swings for a while..."

t_ch: 这里不安全。
t_en: It's not safe here.

t_ch: 还好来的是我们！要是被娜塔莎捉到，你们就等着挨罚吧。
t_en: You should be happy it's us and not Natasha! If she'd found you, you'd be in big trouble already.

t_ch: 呣…大姐姐说的也有道理。我知道啦，那我先去找尤利安咯。
t_en: Mm... I guess so. Anyway, I'm gonna go find Julian.

t_ch: 虎克！嘿嘿，又抓到你啦！
t_en: Hook! Hehe, got you again!

t_ch: 怎、怎么是你们！可恶——尤利安这家伙，居然还请外援？真是不讲武德！
t_en: Huh!? Wh—What the heck are you guys doing here? Ugh, wait till I get my hands on Julian, that traitor... Where's his integrity!?

t_ch: 老实说吧，是不是你带着小孩子们跑来铆钉镇上的？
t_en: What about your integrity, huh? You brought the other kids out here to Rivet Town, didn't you?

t_ch: 我…才、才不是！探险活动的目的地是鼹鼠党的成员一起决定的！
t_en: I... I did not! The Moles hold a vote on all our expedition destinations!

t_ch: 集合鼹鼠党吧，我们有事要宣布！
t_en: Gather your minions, Hook. We've got exciting news!

t_ch: 行吧，看你们好像挺着急的，这次就由着你们吧！
t_en: ...Fine, I will. But it better be exciting.

t_ch: 该去和娜塔莎告状了…
t_en: Maybe we should go and get Natasha...

t_ch: 虎、虎克最讨厌打小报告的人啦！我劝你们不要做傻事！
t_en: Ah, you snitch! You'll regret it, I'm warning you...

t_ch: 好啦好啦，别贫嘴了，去尤利安那边吧。
t_en: Okay, okay, that's enough of that. Let's go and find Julian.

t_ch: 捉迷藏比赛，列车组对鼹鼠党——二比零！
t_en: Hide-and-seek update... Astral Express crew — 2, The Moles — 0!

t_ch: 可恶，别神气得太早！正经的捉迷藏比赛可是五局三胜哒！
t_en: Hmph, celebrate while you can! Hide-and-seek is best of five, y'know...

t_ch: 想想就来气，不说这个啦！你们跑老远来找鼹鼠党有什么事，快点说吧！
t_en: Anyway, enough about hide-and-seek. You came all this way to tell The Moles something, so spit it out.

t_ch: 嘿嘿…{NICKNAME}，快把好消息告诉他们吧！
t_en: Hehe... {NICKNAME}, do you wanna do the honors?

t_ch: 将胜利的消息分享给了鼹鼠党的孩子们……
t_en: Telling The Moles about the victory...

t_ch: 欸？怎么回事，你们怎么都不说话了？
t_en: Huh? What's wrong? Why aren't you saying anything?

t_ch: 虎克，尤利安……
t_en: Hook, Julian...

t_ch: 这故事有这么恐怖吗…
t_en: Is our story that scary?

t_ch: 我可没料到会是这个反应…
t_en: I wasn't expecting this reaction...

t_ch: 呜…呜……
t_en: *sob*

t_ch: …好、好啦！不许哭，尤利安！你可是鼹鼠党的二把手，才不能给大人看笑话！
t_en: ...H—Hey! Wipe your tears, Julian! You're second-in-command — no crying in front of grown-ups!

t_ch: 哼，你们三个，给我记住！从今天起，你们就是鼹鼠党最大的竞争对手！
t_en: Hmph, you three listen up. Starting from today, you are The Moles' number one rivals!

t_ch: 竞争对手…？什么呀，没头没尾的，完全听不懂你在说什么嘛！
t_en: Rivals...? Why the heck are we rivals now? What are you talking about?

t_ch: …虎克和尤利安，他们一直以来的梦想，就是有朝一日能打败上面的坏人，带着下层大家夺回家园。
t_en: ...Hook and Julian always wanted to defeat the villains on the surface and take back our homeland.

t_ch: 现在，这个风头都被哥哥姐姐们抢走了，所以……
t_en: But it sounds like you grown-ups just stole their thunder, so...

t_ch: 呜…我、我们才不会轻易认输！总有一天，鼹鼠党要当上下层区的大英雄，让大家都对我们刮…刮……
t_en: Hmph! You think we're gonna accept defeat just like that!? One day The Moles will be the heroes of the Underworld and the Overworld! Then everyone will sit... sit...

t_ch: 刮目相看！
t_en: Sit up and take notice!

t_ch: 刮骨去毒？
t_en: Sit down and shut up!

t_ch: …是刮目相看才对吧！
t_en: ...Sit up and take notice, right?

t_ch: 啊，对——刮目相看！我、我能想起来的，才不需要你们提醒！
t_en: Ah! Yeah — sit up and take notice! I—I would've figured it out myself. I didn't need you to remind me!

t_ch: 半天不说话，原来你们是在想这事呀。嘿嘿，想变得跟我们一样厉害，虎克可要每天都坚持锻炼哦。
t_en: So that's why you were so silent! Hmph, if you wanna be as awesome as us, you better start training every day~

t_ch: …还要多听娜塔莎的话。
t_en: ...And start listening to Natasha.

t_ch: 坚持锻炼…听老巫婆的话…这样就能变得比你们更强吗？虎克知道了！
t_en: Training every day... listening to the old witch... Will that really make us as awesome as you guys? In that case... deal!

t_ch: 走吧，尤利安，阿丽娜！我们跑步回镇上去，锻炼计划就从今天开始吧！
t_en: Julian, Alina, let's run back to the town! Training starts today!

t_ch: 是、是！我明白了，老大！
t_en: Y—Yes boss! Understood!

t_ch: 阿、阿丽娜，你要是跑不动的话，我可以背你回去……
t_en: Uh, Alina, I can... uh... give you a piggyback if you can't make it...

t_ch: 快看，帕斯卡在那里！在它旁边的是……
t_en: Look, Pascal's over there! Who's standing next to it?

t_ch: 咦？那不是「灰熊」型的机器人吗？我们快去看看！
t_en: Huh? Isn't that a Grizzly robot? Let's go take a look!

t_ch: 帕斯卡！你怎么伤成这个样子了……？
t_en: Pascal! Who did this to you?

t_ch: 请远离，克拉拉。该个体为裂界生物，危险等级评估——极高。
t_en: Clara, get away. This is a Fragmentum creature. It's extremely dangerous.

t_ch: 裂界怪物？！你在说什么呀……
t_en: A Fragmentum creature!? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 这明明是你的同类！
t_en: This is clearly a robot, just like you!

t_ch: 裂界生物都进化到这种程度了？
t_en: Have Fragmentum creatures evolved to this level?

t_ch: 怎么可能…帕斯卡明明就是机器人，是你的同类呀！
t_en: How is that possible? Pascal is obviously a robot, just like you!

t_ch: 是不是史瓦罗派你来的？快说！
t_en: Did Svarog send you? Spit it out!

t_ch: 本机正在执行例行巡逻任务，并未收到来自机械网络中枢的其他指令。
t_en: I am on a standard patrol mission. I have not received any other orders from the network hub.

t_ch: 它说得应该没错…史瓦罗先生向克拉拉作出了保证，绝对不会出尔反尔的。
t_en: It's telling the truth... Mr. Svarog made a promise to me, and he never betrays his promises.

t_ch: 难道是发生了什么故障…可它看上去很正常啊？
t_en: Could it have malfunctioned? It appears to be normal...

t_ch: 请退后，本机将对该裂界生物进行无害化处理——
t_en: Please step back. I shall eliminate this Fragmentum creature...

t_ch: …不好，它要向帕斯卡发起攻击了！必须阻止它才行！
t_en: No, it's going to attack Pascal! We have to stop it!

t_ch: 侦测到新增敌意反应——剿灭开始。
t_en: Hostility detected. Commence elimination protocols.

t_ch: 糟糕！它好像把我们也当作敌人了……
t_en: Oh no! It seems to think we're its enemies as well...

t_ch: 咻…还好，这台巡逻机器人和帕斯卡的核心都保住了，这个大家伙的外壳也几乎没有受损…谢谢{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}。
t_en: Phew... Luckily, the patrol robot and Pascal's cores are still intact. The big fella's shell is only slightly dented... Thank you for your help.

t_ch: 只有一点让克拉拉感到很奇怪——大家伙为什么会把帕斯卡错认成裂界生物呢？
t_en: The only thing that puzzles me is why the big fella mistook Pascal for a Fragmentum creature.

t_ch: 唔，想不明白…先不想了。
t_en: Hmm, I can't figure it out... Well, let's not worry about that now.

t_ch: 总之，这颗核心，克拉拉先带回去，之后再检查一下。我们先把帕斯卡的核心移植到大家伙的外壳里吧。
t_en: Anyway, I'll take this core back and examine it. Now, let's insert Pascal's core into the big fella's shell.

t_ch: 检测，确认，各模块，正常，功能，连接。
t_en: Testing, verifying, all modules, working, connected.

t_ch: 欢迎，大家，回来，终于。帕斯卡，一直，等待！
t_en: Welcome, back, everyone, finally. Pascal, has been, waiting!

t_ch: 帕斯卡——你终于恢复正常了！
t_en: Pascal, you're back to normal!

t_ch: 完美的结局！该结算奖励了。
t_en: A happy ending! We should work out our rewards.

t_ch: 不对，还有一件事…我们还要向帕斯卡问清楚——它到底为什么要偷零件。
t_en: Um... No, there's one more thing... We still need to find out why Pascal was stealing parts in the first place.

t_ch: 问问它为什么要偷零件吧。
t_en: Ask him why he was stealing parts.

t_ch: 啊…克拉拉差点忘记了。谢谢{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}的提醒！
t_en: Oh, I almost forgot. Thank you for reminding me!

t_ch: 呃…现在该做什么？
t_en: Erm... what's next?

t_ch: 唔…我们还要向帕斯卡问清楚——它到底为什么要偷零件。
t_en: Hmm... We still need to ask Pascal why he was stealing parts in the first place.

t_ch: 帕斯卡…现在，能请你告诉我，为什么要偷零件吗……？
t_en: Pascal... Could you please, uh... explain why you were stealing parts...?

t_ch: ——零件，收集，返回，工坊。
t_en: Collecting, parts, returning to, workshop.

t_ch: 工坊？是指铆钉镇上那座「淬火工坊」吗？
t_en: Workshop? Oh, do you mean the Tempering Workshop in Rivet Town?

t_ch: 一直，等待，修缮。跟随，请，帕斯卡！
t_en: Been, waiting, repair. Follow, please, Pascal!

t_ch: 啊…帕斯卡自顾自地跑掉了！它是要我们跟上它吗？
t_en: Ah... Pascal ran off!

t_ch: 往那个方向去了…我们快跟上去看看吧？
t_en: He went in that direction... I think he wants us to follow him!

t_ch: 这里往下继续走…好像是废弃餐厅？
t_en: Up ahead... Is that an abandoned restaurant?

t_ch: 这里…似乎是铆钉镇的废弃餐厅呢。克拉拉曾经在这里找到了不少罐头…营地里的叔叔阿姨们为此开心了很久。
t_en: I think this place used to be a restaurant. One time, I found a bunch of canned food here... The vagrants in the camp were so happy.

t_ch: 不过，克拉拉一直在好奇一件事……
t_en: However, I've always wondered...

t_ch: 这里，为什么…灯光从来没断过呢？明明已经被废弃了……
t_en: The light here... Why is it still on? This place has clearly been abandoned for a long time...

t_ch: 而且，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}你看——这座餐厅虽然到处是灰尘…但设施似乎都没有老化的迹象呢。
t_en: Also, there's dust all over the place, but none of the equipment appears to be deteriorating.

t_ch: 这个问题也困扰了我很久…
t_en: I've been wondering about that too...

t_ch: 难道有人住在这里？
t_en: Is it possible that someone lives here?

t_ch: 仔细想想…这里应该不会有人住。至少克拉拉在这里寻找物资的时候，从来没见到过。
t_en: I don't think so... No one should be living here. I never saw anyone when I came here to hunt for supplies.

t_ch: 可能是铆钉镇闹鬼吧…
t_en: Maybe Rivet Town is haunted...

t_ch: 咿？！…那岂不是，我们现在就……
t_en: Ah!? Are you saying we're... not alone...?

t_ch: 不对不对…这个世界上才没有那种东西……
t_en: No, no, no... Ghosts don't exist...

t_ch: 唔，总之…大概是有人一直在修缮这里吧？可到底是谁呢……
t_en: Anyway, someone must be looking after this place. But who?

t_ch: 而且…为什么要做这种事呢？
t_en: And why...?

t_ch: 帕斯卡的痕迹往那边去了…我们顺着它继续前进吧。
t_en: Pascal's trail leads in that direction. Let's follow it.

t_ch: 这里就是「淬火工坊」…帕斯卡果然在这里。
t_en: Hey, this is the Tempering Workshop. And look, Pascal is here.

t_ch: 克拉拉，欢迎，铆钉镇，回到，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Clara, welcome, back, Rivet Town!

t_ch: 看到，是否，亮堂堂？工作，帕斯卡，完成，圆满！
t_en: Look, isn't it, shiny? Job, Pascal, done, nice!

t_ch: 咦？难道…是帕斯卡一直在修缮这座废弃的城镇吗？
t_en: Huh? Could it be... Pascal has been repairing this abandoned town?

t_ch: 帕斯卡，修缮，确实！等待，一直，大家。
t_en: Pascal, repair, indeed! Waiting, been, everyone.

t_ch: 这就是你偷零件的原因？
t_en: Is this why you've been stealing the parts?

t_ch: 零件，帕斯卡，收集，不停。铆钉镇，修缮，不停！
t_en: Parts, Pascal, collect, non-stop. Rivet Town, repair, non-stop!

t_ch: 看来，帕斯卡之所以一直在偷零件…是因为它要修缮这座城镇呢。
t_en: It seems that Pascal was stealing parts because he wants to repair the town.

t_ch: 你一直在等我们吗？
t_en: Have you been waiting for us?

t_ch: 等待，大家，帕斯卡，不停！离开，大家，回来，有一天！
t_en: Waiting, everyone, Pascal, non-stop! Leave, everyone, come back, one day!

t_ch: 难道…帕斯卡是在等待撤离铆钉镇的居民回到这里吗？
t_en: Has Pascal been waiting for Rivet Town's evacuated residents to return?

t_ch: 你一直在修缮这座城镇？
t_en: Have you been repairing the town?

t_ch: 修缮，一直，每天，帕斯卡！使用，可以，大家，回来！
t_en: Repair, been, every day, Pascal! Use, can, everyone, return!

t_ch: 大概是为了让大家回来后能正常生活…帕斯卡才一直在维护这里吧？
t_en: Pascal has been looking after the town so that everyone can go back to their previous lives when they return...

t_ch: 所以…为什么带我们来这里？
t_en: So... why did you bring us here?

t_ch: 「工坊」，「淬火」，这里。基地，帕斯卡，秘密！
t_en: Workshop, Tempering, here. Base, Pascal, secret!

t_ch: 零件，存放！敌人，躲避。地方，安全。害怕，不用，大家！
t_en: Parts, store! Enemies, not here. Place, safe. Scared, no need, everybody!

t_ch: 看来，这里就是帕斯卡存放零件的秘密基地吧？
t_en: Ahh. This appears to be the secret base where Pascal keeps the parts.

t_ch: 唔，虽然知道了帕斯卡偷零件的原因…但还有许多问题没有解决呢。比如……
t_en: Although we now know why he took them, there are still many unanswered questions. For example...

t_ch: 它修缮城镇的动机是什么？
t_en: Why does he want to repair the town?

t_ch: 是的。但克拉拉觉得，动机大概是没办法直接问出来的……
t_en: Right. But I don't think Pascal will give us a straightforward answer on this one...

t_ch: 唔…不知道这里会不会有线索呢？
t_en: Hmm... I wonder if we can find any clues here?

t_ch: 它为什么会被当作裂界生物？
t_en: Why is Pascal being treated as a Fragmentum creature?

t_ch: …{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}说得对。刚才那个大家伙说的话，让克拉拉很在意……
t_en: ...Right. That bigger robot said that, how strange...

t_ch: 唔…不知道这里会不会有线索呢？
t_en: Hmm... I wonder if we can find any clues here?

t_ch: 该怎么处置这个零件小偷…
t_en: How should we punish this parts thief then...?

t_ch: …{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}说得对。虽然帕斯卡是出于好意才……
t_en: ...Right. Pascal is doing this out of good will, but still...

t_ch: 但无论如何，偷东西肯定是不对的，必须让帕斯卡明白这点。
t_en: Stealing is always wrong, and Pascal needs to learn that.

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我们在附近再多看看吧？克拉拉还有些在意的事。
t_en: Let's take a look around again, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}. I'm still not finished here.

t_ch: 铆钉镇，居住，克拉拉，{NICKNAME}！游览，随意，请！
t_en: Rivet Town, live, Clara, {NICKNAME}! Tour, feel free, please!

t_ch: 经过精心保养的显像管显示器。虽然有些许灰尘覆盖其上，但似乎还能正常工作。
t_en: A well maintained CRT display device. Though covered by some dust, it still functions.

t_ch: 这应该是帕斯卡仔细维护的成果…不过，这个小家伙应该没有使用显示器的需要吧。
t_en: This must be the result of Pascal's meticulous repairs... but the little fella doesn't really need the monitor, does he?

t_ch: 唔…机柜侧边好像有些人类的手掌印呢。难道最近有人用过这台显示器？
t_en: Hmm... Hey! Those look like handprints on the side of the cabinet — human handprints. Could this monitor have been recently used by someone?

t_ch: 书页与信纸被仔细裁剪，摞于一处。内容从幼儿寓言故事至过去的时政要闻，不一而足。
t_en: Book pages and letters all carefully cut and put aside, whose content varies from tales for children to outdated political news.

t_ch: 这些书页，裁得好整齐…啊，还有一股地髓燃料的味道。
t_en: These pages are so neatly cut out... Oh, and they smell like Geomarrow Fuel.

t_ch: 应该不会有人故意在铆钉镇做这种事…难道是帕斯卡自己收集了这些书页，放在一起了吗？
t_en: Is there someone here in Rivet Town doing this...? Or could Pascal have collected the pages and put them together?

t_ch: 咦？这是什么，是日记本吗？
t_en: Huh? What's this? A diary?

t_ch: 封面上好像有名字呢，好工整的笔迹呀。不对，工整到这个程度，应该是直接印上去的才对……
t_en: Wow. Such neat handwriting... no, wait a minute, I think this was printed onto the cover...

t_ch: …难道是，帕斯卡？
t_en: ...Could it be Pascal's?

t_ch: 帕斯卡……
t_en: Pascal...

t_ch: 这下一切都说得通了…
t_en: It all makes sense now...

t_ch: 事件的原貌居然是这样……
t_en: So that's what happened...

t_ch: …我们接下来该怎么做？
t_en: What should we do now?

t_ch: 我也不知道……
t_en: I don't know...

t_ch: 克拉拉需要好好想想…{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}，我们再把帕斯卡的事从头到尾理一下吧？
t_en: Let me think... How about we go over Pascal's whole story again, from the start?

t_ch: （试着梳理整个事件的脉络）
t_en: (Try to sort out the whole story)

t_ch: （铆钉镇出事后，它的后备能源启动…却发现铆钉镇已经空无一人了。）
t_en: (His backup power was activated after the incident in Rivet Town... and it woke up to discover that Rivet Town had been abandoned.)

t_ch: （它决定收集零件、修缮城镇，等大家回来……）
t_en: (He decided to collect parts, repair the town, and wait for everyone to return...)

t_ch: （…期间，帕斯卡发展出了自我意识。）
t_en: (Around that time, Pascal became sentient.)

t_ch: 你将思考的结果向克拉拉复述了一遍。
t_en: You recapitulate your thoughts to Clara.

t_ch: 是这样的……
t_en: That is correct...

t_ch: 我还…有些糊涂。
t_en: I'm still a little confused...

t_ch: 帕斯卡它…似乎曾经是铆钉镇的护卫机器人之一。
t_en: So Pascal used to be a robot guardian of Rivet Town...

t_ch: 铆钉镇居民撤离后不久，帕斯卡的后备能源因为某种原因启动——
t_en: Pascal's backup power was activated for some reason shortly after the residents were evacuated...

t_ch: 可重新醒来的它发现，铆钉镇已经空无一人了。于是，帕斯卡决定收集零件、修缮城镇，等待大家回来。
t_en: It woke up to discover that the whole place had been abandoned. So, it decided to collect parts, repair the town, and wait for everyone to return...

t_ch: 但是，不知道出于什么原因，帕斯卡频繁遭到同类的攻击…因为自动机兵们会把它识别为裂界生物。
t_en: However, for some reason, Pascal keeps getting attacked by his fellow robots, because the automatons think he's a Fragmentum creature.

t_ch: 或许是它产生了自我意识？
t_en: Maybe Pascal has gained sentience?

t_ch: 莫非真是被裂界生物附体了…？
t_en: Could it be possessed by a Fragmentum creature...?

t_ch: …克拉拉也不清楚。但我猜，帕斯卡的自我意识之所以会如此混乱，肯定和频频遭受同类攻击有关系。
t_en: I'm not sure, but I suspect Pascal's tormented sense of self stems from him being constantly attacked by his fellow robots.

t_ch: 用于标识「情感」的模组不断受损…到了最后，它就变成了只知道不停收集零件的小机器人。
t_en: The module used to detect emotions was frequently damaged, eventually turning him into a small robot that did nothing but collect parts.

t_ch: 这或许就是事件的全貌了。
t_en: That's probably what happened.

t_ch: 至少克拉拉觉得是这样。但帕斯卡究竟为什么会被当作裂界生物？我还找不到答案……
t_en: At least, that's my theory. Still, that doesn't explain why Pascal is being treated as a Fragmentum creature...

t_ch: 它为什么会被当作裂界生物？
t_en: Why is Pascal being treated as a Fragmentum creature?

t_ch: 不知道…从帕斯卡的日志里找不到答案。
t_en: I don't know... I can't find the answer in Pascal's diary.

t_ch: 帕斯卡，到底为什么你会被同伴嫌弃……
t_en: Pascal, why do your peers keep rejecting you...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，克拉拉，游览，愉快，是否？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Clara, tour, enjoyable, is it?

t_ch: 帕斯卡，收集零件，准备，继续！或，等待，指令，下一步！
t_en: Pascal, collect parts, ready, continue! Or, wait, instruction, next!

t_ch: 看来…帕斯卡的自我意识，还没有恢复到日志中表现出的高度……
t_en: Pascal's consciousness does not appear to have returned to the level shown in the diary...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，克拉拉，铆钉镇，居民！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Clara, Rivet Town, resident!

t_ch: 帕斯卡，保护，大家！
t_en: Pascal, protect, everybody!

t_ch: …我明白了。谢谢你，帕斯卡。
t_en: I see. Thank you so much, Pascal.

t_ch: 帕斯卡，听克拉拉的话——你可以先停止收集零件了。你就乖乖待在这里，等我们回来给你下一步指令…好吗？
t_en: You can stop gathering parts for the time being. Just stay here and wait for us to return and give you your next orders, alright?

t_ch: ——帕斯卡，收到！乖乖，等待！
t_en: Pascal, affirmative! Be good, wait!

t_ch: {F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}——我们…还是先回史瓦罗先生那里讨论一下吧。
t_en: {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother}, let's go back to Mr. Svarog and talk about what to do next.

t_ch: 娜塔！我们回来了。
t_en: Nat! We're back.

t_ch: 不愧是希儿，永远都这么准时。怎么样，你们找到能用的物资了吗？
t_en: Hehe, that's our Seele. Nothing if not punctual. So, did you find any usable supplies?

t_ch: 我来看看…钢板、纱布、止痛剂、医用酒精……太好了，需要的物资都齐全了。
t_en: Let me see... metal plates, gauze bandages, painkillers, rubbing alcohol... Perfect, that's everything I needed.

t_ch: 就是止痛剂的分量稍微少了些，储藏室那边出了什么情况吗？
t_en: Huh... It's just, there are less painkillers than I'd expected. Did someone break into the storage room?

t_ch: 分给了克拉拉一些。
t_en: We shared some with Clara.

t_ch: …克拉拉？
t_en: ...Clara?

t_ch: 被怪物毁掉了一些。
t_en: Monsters had destroyed some of them.

t_ch: 喂，用不着说谎，娜塔不会因为这种事埋怨咱们的。
t_en: Hey, no need to lie, Nat wouldn't be upset about what we did.

t_ch: 是克拉拉，她比我们先一步到了储藏室。止痛剂我们跟她平分了。
t_en: Clara had already arrived at the storage room by the time we got there. We split the painkillers with her.

t_ch: 那孩子的话，我大概明白…她是想照顾受伤的流浪者吧？克拉拉一直很善良……
t_en: ...If she was there too then... I understand. She wants to look after the injured vagrants, right? Clara's always been a kind-hearted girl...

t_ch: 我认为你们做了正确的决定。不过这么一来，我得好好规划下用药的剂量了。
t_en: I think you made the right decision. Still, it means I'm going to have to draw up a detailed dosage plan.

t_ch: 不怕，有我帮你。
t_en: Hey, don't worry. I'll help you.

t_ch: 嘿…不用啦，诊所交给我就好。你们三个还有更重要的事要做吧？奥列格——还有那两位小伙伴，他们正等着呢。
t_en: Um, no need. I'll handle the clinic. I assume you three have more important things to be getting on with, right? Oleg will be waiting, not to mention your two companions.

t_ch: 都快忘了还有这俩人了。
t_en: I'd nearly forgotten about those two.

t_ch: 原来你们三个关系没那么好啊？我还以为是生死之交呢。
t_en: So you guys aren't that close after all? I thought you were sworn friends.

t_ch: 哈哈，布洛妮娅真可爱呢。{NICKNAME}明显是在开玩笑，对吧？
t_en: Haha, you're adorable, Bronya. {NICKNAME} was obviously joking~

t_ch: 走之前忘了跟他们打招呼了…
t_en: I forgot to say anything to them before I left...

t_ch: 倒也没太大关系，我看那两个孩子精神得很呢。
t_en: I'm sure they won't mind, they seemed in good spirits.

t_ch: 奥列格大叔是想谈谈史瓦罗的事吧？我们得走了，不好意思，娜塔——伤员就麻烦你啦。
t_en: I bet old Oleg wants to talk about Svarog. We should get going — sorry Nat, we'll have to leave the wounded to you.

t_ch: 放心吧，祝你们顺利啊。
t_en: No worries, I hope things go smoothly.
*yawn*

t_ch: 怎么，这就困了？
t_en: Huh? Tired already?

t_ch: 你不累吗？一晚上没休息了。
t_en: Aren't you? We didn't get any rest last night.

t_ch: 我的最高纪录可是六天不合眼，你还得加把劲啊。
t_en: You're gonna have to step it up, y'know. My record is six days no shut-eye.

t_ch: 嘿！他们总算回来啦。
t_en: Hey, they're finally back!

t_ch: 没打招呼就消失了，抱歉。
t_en: Sorry for the unannounced disappearance.

t_ch: 哈，这有什么，不打招呼就消失的纪录保持者在这儿呢！
t_en: Ha, that's nothing — the unannounced disappearance record-holder is right here!

t_ch: 不知道你骄傲什么。
t_en: And that's something to be proud of?

t_ch: 别担心，我毫发无损。
t_en: Don't worry, I'm completely unscathed.

t_ch: …呃，我没担心你。要说的话，我也是担心布洛妮娅呀。
t_en: ...Uh, I wasn't worried about you. If you must know, I was wondering if Bronya was okay.

t_ch: 你明明担心得坐立不安。
t_en: Oh please — you've barely sat still since they left.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这下人都齐了，那我就直入主题——
t_en: Hahaha, it's good to have everyone back together. Let me get straight to it...

t_ch: 关于你们上次说的「星核」，还有在上层区的遭遇，这两位年轻人又从头帮我捋了一遍。
t_en: Regarding this "Stellaron" and your predicament in the Overworld, your friends have gone over both with me again.

t_ch: 上次见到可可利亚，她还是个做派强势的小丫头…没想到当上守护者以后……
t_en: The last time I saw Cocolia, she was an impressive young lady... I never thought that after becoming the guardian...

t_ch: 只能说，我对你们的遭遇表示同情。放心吧，「地火」不会干背后捅刀子的事儿。
t_en: All I can say is that I sympathize with your situation. Rest easy. Wildfire won't go behind your back on this.

t_ch: 虽然你们的计划跟矿工喝醉以后的胡言乱语差不多，但至少指了条路。下层人早已无路可走，事已至此，我宁愿在你们身上赌一把。
t_en: Your plan sounds a bit like the ravings of a drunken miner, but at least you're offering a road forwards. We Underworlders haven't had a road to anywhere for a long time now, and you can see how things have become. I'm willing to give you guys a shot.

t_ch: 放心吧大叔，我们会证明你没信错人！
t_en: Don't worry, Mr. Oleg, we'll make sure you don't regret it!

t_ch: 也不是矿工喝醉以后的胡言乱语。
t_en: Ravings of a drunken miner is a little harsh.

t_ch: 好！那就把目光放得现实一点儿，你们几个想知道「星核」的下落，而我们「地火」想解除下层区的封锁——
t_en: Maybe it is! But I think we need to set our sights on something a bit more realistic. You guys want to know the whereabouts of the Stellaron. Wildfire wants to remove the restrictions on the Underworld...

t_ch: 也就是说，我们的目标都是史瓦罗。不解决那个「大佬」，后面的东西都是废话。
t_en: In other words, Svarog is the target of both our efforts. If we can't deal with the boss, we can't deal with anything.

t_ch: 没错，我们去打倒史瓦罗！
t_en: You're right. Let's go take him down!

t_ch: 先礼后兵，还是先考虑和平沟通吧。
t_en: Diplomacy before violence. We should try peaceful communication first.

t_ch: 你俩说的都有道理，我们当然想尽量和平解决问题，避免损失。但是假如情况有变，那我们也得时刻做好使用武力的准备。
t_en: Both your approaches have merit. We would always rather avoid losses and settle matters through peaceful means. But if the situation changes, we must be prepared for armed conflict at a moment's notice.

t_ch: 得想办法让他站在我们一边。
t_en: We need to find a way to bring him over to our side.

t_ch: 哼，他是个机器人，听得懂什么？直接砍翻那坨废铁最快了！
t_en: Hmph, he wouldn't understand — you're forgetting he's a robot. It'd be faster to smash him to scrap metal!

t_ch: 没错，我们得做好两手准备。能和平解决问题当然最好，但假如情况有变，也得时刻做好使用武力的准备。
t_en: We should make preparations for both approaches. Of course, it's better to settle matters through peaceful means. But if the situation changes, we must be prepared for armed conflict at a moment's notice.

t_ch: 「地火」多次尝试跟史瓦罗接触。但每次都被拒之门外，那家伙压根不打算跟我们对话。
t_en: Wildfire has tried many times to make contact with Svarog — without success. He has no intention of engaging with us.

t_ch: 以我们的实力，来硬的风险极大。史瓦罗的机器人不怕牺牲，我却不想拿下层百姓的命冒险。
t_en: At our current strength, it would be incredibly dangerous to use force. Svarog's robots don't fear sacrifice, and I don't want to risk Underworld lives.

t_ch: 但现在不一样了，你们的到来让史瓦罗也承认「出现外来变量」，事情发生了变化，我虽然不清楚原委——但也许你们有机会说动他。
t_en: But things have changed — your arrival constitutes an "external variable" for Svarog. I may not have the full picture, but perhaps you guys have an opportunity to sway him.

t_ch: 那是最好。
t_en: That would be best.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈！瞧咱们这小算盘打的？想得是真美呵。唉，等你们真到了史瓦罗的地盘就懂了，这地下从来就没有那么顺利的事儿。
t_en: Hahahaha! Our scheme is coming along nicely! But I fear that Svarog just isn't the swaying type. *sigh* You'll understand when you get to his territory. Things never run smoothly down here.

t_ch: 怎么说，铁卫丫头？我们这一圈人里，就你没有必须跟史瓦罗死磕到底的理由。我倒想听听你的打算。
t_en: What about you, Miss Silvermane? You're the only one here that isn't compelled to do or die with Svarog. I'd like to hear your plan.

t_ch: 下层区也是贝洛伯格的一部分，如果史瓦罗的存在威胁到了这里的居民，我当然站在你们这一边。
t_en: The Underworld is still a part of Belobog. If Svarog's existence constitutes a threat to the people here, I will of course stand with you.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，很好！不愧是要接过守护者担子的姑娘，觉悟很高啊！
t_en: Hahaha, excellent! An enlightened answer, young lady — and spoken like a true successor to the guardianship.

t_ch: 既然我们统一战线，就没必要再耽搁了。走吧，我安排了向导带你们去找史瓦罗。
t_en: Seeing as we're all on the same side, there's no reason to delay. Let's go, I've arranged for a guide to take you to Svarog.

t_ch: 你不一起来吗？
t_en: You're not coming?

t_ch: 那「地火」在干嘛？
t_en: What will Wildfire be doing?

t_ch: 「地火」一向跟史瓦罗交恶，跟着你们只怕引起误会，帮了倒忙。我们会在不远处待命，如果事态不对，「地火」立刻赶到。
t_en: Wildfire's relationship with Svarog has been hostile for a long time now, accompanying you would only lead to misunderstandings. We'll be nearby, awaiting orders. If things go south, we'll be there in no time.

t_ch: 希儿，再带外来者朋友一程吧。
t_en: Seele, perhaps you can take our outsider friends the rest of the way.

t_ch: 交给我吧，头儿。
t_en: Mm, I'm on it, boss.

t_ch: 希儿，大叔说的向导不是你吗？
t_en: So you're not the guide Mr. Oleg was talking about, Seele?

t_ch: 不是，奥列格没交代过这事。但这个向导是谁，我应该猜得八九不离十……
t_en: No, Oleg didn't assign that to me. But I've got a pretty good idea who it's gonna be.

t_ch: 桑博呗。向导啊行商啊，骗子啊魔术师啊，这种活简直量身给他打造的。
t_en: I guess Sampo. Businessman, magician, liar, and now guide... This kind of work was made for him.

t_ch: 嗯…嗯……
t_en: Huh... Hm...

t_ch: …你干嘛一直发出奇怪的声音？
t_en: ...You okay over there?

t_ch: 没啥，只是觉得一宿不见，大家的氛围好像变好了呢……
t_en: It's nothing, it just feels like people are getting along better since your unannounced disappearance...

t_ch: …虎克，是你？
t_en: ...Hook? You...

t_ch: 这我是真的没想到……
t_en: This I really wasn't expecting...

t_ch: 原来如此，鼹鼠党也是「地火」的……
t_en: So, The Moles are a part of Wildfire...

t_ch: 是、是我漆黑的虎克没错！找我有事吗？
t_en: Th—That's right! It's me, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great! What do you want?

t_ch: 呃，小朋友，应该是误会……
t_en: Uh, kiddo, I think there's been a misunderstanding...

t_ch: 是我啊，朋友们，奥列格派我来给你们带个路……
t_en: I will be your guide, friends. By order of Chief Oleg...

t_ch: 实在太让我伤心了…你们连这么小的孩子都想到了，却没想起我……
t_en: I gotta say I'm a little upset at the thought of you choosing a toddler over your old buddy Sampo...

t_ch: 行了行了，计较这个干嘛。我倒想问你：「地火」谁都知道史瓦罗的老巢在哪儿，要你这个向导干嘛？
t_en: Alright, alright, don't get started. But I wanna ask you something: Everyone in Wildfire knows the location of Svarog's lair. Why do we need you as a guide?

t_ch: 嗐，术业有专攻呗。「地火」虽然知道史瓦罗的根据地在哪儿，可从来没进去过啊。
t_en: *sigh* My specialties in the field, perhaps? Wildfire may know where the base is, but have they ever been inside?

t_ch: 你进去过？
t_en: You've been inside?

t_ch: …没有。但那机械聚落上下我摸了个遍，收集了不少线索，保证能帮上你们的大忙。
t_en: ...No. But I've felt my way around the outside and picked up more than a few clues. I can help you. You have my word.

t_ch: 希望你言行一致。
t_en: Let's hope you can walk the talk.

t_ch: 哎哟，这叫什么话！天底下还有比我桑博更言行一致的人吗？
t_en: Hey, what are you trying to say!? Name me someone above or below ground that walks the talk like Sampo Koski.

t_ch: 奥列格居然信任你…
t_en: Oleg put his trust in you...?

t_ch: 哎哟，这叫什么话！你们仨能跟「地火」打上交道，不是多亏了老桑博引荐嘛？
t_en: Hey, what are you trying to say!? You guys wouldn't have even gotten to know Wildfire without my help, right?

t_ch: 你没事摸人家聚落干啥。
t_en: Clues for your own shady dealings, no doubt.

t_ch: 哎哟，这叫什么话！那不是「地火」下了需求，我兢兢业业给他办事嘛？
t_en: Hey! That's not nice! Wildfire made a request and I got on with the job.

t_ch: 天地可鉴，我桑博真是一片热忱——
t_en: My legacy will prove that Sampo Koski was nothing but a willing and devoted servant of—

t_ch: 行了行了！我们信你还不行嘛，快点带路吧。
t_en: Ugh, enough! Jeez! We believe you, okay? Show us the way already.

t_ch: ——好嘞！那你们可跟紧了。
t_en: Great! Stay close.

t_ch: 这是史瓦罗的老巢？我还以为是个冷冰冰的地方，居然这么热闹？
t_en: This is Svarog's lair? I thought it'd be cold and lifeless, turns out it's pretty busy!

t_ch: 很多流浪者都暂住在这里，他们的老家被裂界的怪物占据了。
t_en: Many vagrants are staying here temporarily. Their homes were overrun by monsters.

t_ch: 在史瓦罗的眼皮底下扎营，不觉得危险吗？
t_en: Isn't it dangerous for them to make camp right under Svarog's metal nose?

t_ch: 不会，史瓦罗虽然霸道，但从不无故攻击人类。对无家可归的人来说，这地方反而安全。
t_en: No. Svarog may be ruthless, but he never attacks without reason. For those with no home, this is actually a safe place to be.

t_ch: 呵…要不是搬走得早，说不定我现在也是这群人中的一员哪。
t_en: Hm... If I hadn't moved away when I did, I'd probably be where they are right now.

t_ch: 哎呀呀，这地方可真是泥沙俱下，啥人都有。招子放亮点伙计们，别被这些家伙给骗了啊。

t_ch: 你不骗我们就谢天谢地了。
t_en: It's all good. We've had you for practice.

t_ch: 小姑娘，你可真记仇。
t_en: Y'know missy, you sure know how to bear a grudge.

t_ch: 那是什么，密码吗？
t_en: Is that a secret code or something?

t_ch: 童话故事里的口令，在这里不可能奏效吧。
t_en: It's an imaginary password that children like to use... and I don't think it's going to be of much use.

t_ch: 说好的线索呢？
t_en: What about these clues of yours?

t_en: Certification?

t_en: Correct, only {RUBY_B#Svarog's Scavengers}prospectors{RUBY_E#} with the right certification can get in or out. These prospectors collect mechanical remnants from all over the underground, and then bring them to Svarog for him to repair.

t_en: Apart from them keeping a watchful eye on these hooligans, another one of their duties is "processing." Anyone who wants to become a {RUBY_B#Svarog's Scavengers}prospector{RUBY_E#} has to pass a test from these walkin' stoplights first.

t_en: You don't know the answer? That's lame. It's back to school for you!

t_ch: 不不不，微晶单元不能插在散热基板上。
t_en: No, no, no! You can't insert the microcrystalline unit into the heat sink substrate like that.

t_ch: 谁说的？「矿灯」巴列维提过，要从古代遗物的接口去考虑它的零件拼合。
t_en: How do you know? Balaway from Miner's Lamp said that you need to pay attention to the interfaces when assembling relics.

t_ch: 要让机器动起来，微晶单元得与操作制动模块接上，不然机器就还是一坨废铁。
t_en: To make this thing move again, we need to connect the microcrystalline unit to the operating brake module. Otherwise, it's just a pile of scrap metal.

t_ch: 你信巴列维可就完蛋了，这老笨蛋，上次这么做他就被电了个人仰马翻的。
t_en: If you believe a word that senile idiot Balaway says, you're done for. The last time he acted on his own advice, he electrocuted himself.

t_ch: 兄弟，你得请教真正的聪明人。要谈怎么修好机器，没人比马雷夫更懂行。
t_en: You should be asking people with real smarts. No one is better at fixing machines than Marev.

t_ch: 我亲眼瞧见过他把微晶导体单元接上逻辑控制中枢，点亮了一台振动钻头。
t_en: I saw him connecting a microcrystalline conductor unit to a logic control hub to get a vibrating drill to work.

t_ch: 马雷夫？他跟他弟弟连古代遗物的真假都分不清。我听说他被个卖假货的娘娘腔骗了。
t_en: Haha, Marev!? Him and his brother wouldn't know a real relic if it hit 'em in the face. Last I heard, he got scammed by someone selling counterfeit junk.

t_ch: 你这是凭空污人清白！
t_en: Ahh, you're just salty, my friend!

t_ch: …饶命，饶命！我知道错了，我这就给你们认证！
t_en: ...Wait, have mercy! I'm sorry! One certification, coming right up!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这才像话嘛！
t_en: Hehe, now we're talkin'!

t_ch: 来，把右手伸出来……
t_en: Okay, please extend your right hand...

t_ch: （伸手）
t_en: (Put hand out)

t_ch: 不是你，是那边的小姑娘！
t_en: Not you, the girl over there!

t_ch: 欸？怎么又是我？很痛的好不好！
t_en: Huh? Me again? It hurts, y'know!

t_ch: 三月，伸手。
t_en: March, put your hand out.

t_ch: 欸？怎么又是我？很痛的好不好！
t_en: Huh? Me again? It hurts, y'know!

t_ch: ——哎哟喂，疼疼疼！
t_en: ...Ah! Owwww!

t_ch: 行了，这下就差一步认证了。你们快走吧，让我一个机器人呆着……
t_en: There you are, only one more to go. Now, please leave me in peace...
Beep... Boop...

t_ch: 不管怎么样，先让它识别一下之前的认证吧。
t_en: Whatever the case, we'll need it to identify our certifications.

t_ch: 这家伙怪怪的……
t_en: There's something wrong with this one...

t_ch: 协议…启启启启启……
t_en: Protocol... Inish-nish-nish-nish-nish...

t_ch: 启动失败。尝试修复复复复复……
t_en: Initialization failure. Attempting system restorashhhh...

t_ch: 不妙……
t_en: Not encouraging...

t_ch: 修复失败。核心模组遭到破坏，发现外部威胁。
t_en: System restoration failure. Core module damaged. External threat detected.

t_ch: 准备清除威胁，战斗模组启动。
t_en: Threat elimination activated. Initializing combat module.

t_ch: 哈！终于不用费脑子了，让我来修理修理这个铁疙瘩！
t_en: Ha! Finally, no more racking our brains. This rust bucket's about to meet its maker!

t_ch: 嘿！这下老实了吧？
t_en: Ha! Learned how to behave now?

t_ch: 我试试重置它的系统……
t_en: Let me try and reset its system...

t_ch: …认证系统已成功上线。请伸出右手。
t_en: ...Certification system online. Please extend your right hand.

t_ch: 唉…又要本姑娘遭罪了……
t_en: *sigh* ...Why is it always me...

t_ch: 哎哟！这下怎么比之前还疼啊！还好这是最后一步了……
t_en: Aaaah!!! How come it hurt even more than the other two!? Hmph, at least that's the final step...

t_ch: 这下咱们就能进去了吧？嘿嘿，史瓦罗的认证机制也不过如此！
t_en: We can get in now, right? Hehe, Svarog's certification system is no match for us!

t_ch: 这玩意到底好不好用？快点回大门那试试吧。
t_en: This better work. Let's get back to the gate and give it a go.

t_ch: 芝～麻～开～门～！
t_en: Opennnn sesame~!

t_ch: 瞧，桑博有用吧？奥列格头儿早就知道——
t_en: See? Not so bad having Sampo on the team after all, right? Chief Oleg knew all along that I—

t_ch: 对对对，多亏了你。
t_en: Yeah, yeah, thank you very much.

t_ch: 我会跟首领表扬你的，现在少说几句吧。
t_en: I'll commend you to the chief. No need to waste your time.

t_ch: 好吧，我就叮嘱一句：这后面可就是未知的领域了，各位可千万小心翼翼，注意脚下……
t_en: Alright, well let me issue a warning now: Everything from here on out is uncharted territory — we must proceed with the utmost caution...

t_ch: 走吧！去找史瓦罗把事情说明白。
t_en: Now to set the record straight with Svarog. Let's get in there!

t_ch: …喂喂，别把我的话当耳旁风啊？
t_en: ...Ugh! Did anyone hear what I just said?
A gate...

t_ch: 见鬼，怎么又是门！
t_en: Ugh... Why is there another gate here!?

t_ch: 看结构，好像比刚才的门还复杂……
t_en: It looks like this gate has a more complicated structure than the last one...

t_ch: 桑博，这是怎么回事？
t_en: Sampo, what is this?

t_ch: …桑博？人呢？
t_en: ...Sampo? Where is he?

t_ch: 欸！那家伙跑哪去了，明明刚才还在啊？
t_en: Ugh! Where did he run off to — he was just here a second ago!

t_ch: …习以为常了。
t_en: ...This isn't the first time.

t_ch: 又是那种机器人！难道想见史瓦罗还得通过其它「试炼」吗？
t_en: It's that same type of robot again! Please don't tell me there's another round of "processing."

t_ch: 发现史瓦罗大佬的访客。
t_en: Visitors for Svarog detected.

t_ch: 协议…启动。开始…扫描…校验。
t_en: Protocol initiating... Scanning... Verifying.

t_ch: 乖，给你认证，让我们进去吧。
t_en: Who's a good boy? Here's our certification, we just wanna see your master.

t_ch: 原来如此！把机器人当作看门狗来对待，妙呀！
t_en: Ooh, good plan! Treat the robots like guard dogs!

t_ch: ——这根本不可能起作用吧！
t_en: ...How is that gonna improve our chances?

t_ch: 小心，不知道这回有什么花招。
t_en: Careful, this one might have a few tricks up its sleeve.

t_ch: 又要动手了吗？好啊，我随时奉陪。
t_en: Are we gonna smash this one too? Count me in.

t_ch: 认证失败…个体未获得访问权限。
t_en: Certification failure... Subject has yet to obtain access permissions.

t_ch: 请求被拒绝。请访客向克拉拉小姐申请访问权限。
t_en: Request rejected. Visitors must acquire access permissions from Miss Clara.

t_ch: 克拉拉？啊，是咱们在大矿区见过的那个小姑娘？
t_en: Clara? Oh, the girl we saw in the Great Mine?

t_ch: 她究竟是什么来头，权限还要问她申请……
t_en: We need to get permissions from her? What's she got to do with this?

t_ch: 她是史瓦罗的家人。
t_en: She's Svarog's family.

t_ch: 她是史瓦罗的主人。
t_en: She's Svarog's owner.

t_ch: 是「家人」吧…你是真记错了还是故意的？
t_en: She's his "family..." Did you really forget?

t_ch: 「家人」…唔，我不是很懂。
t_en: Family... Huh, weird.

t_ch: 别烦恼了，我们去找克拉拉吧。
t_en: Don't trouble yourself about it. We need to find her.

t_ch: …我并无把握，但试试无妨：克拉拉现在在哪？
t_en: ...I don't know if this will work, but it's worth a try: Where is Clara right now?

t_ch: 正在调取音频资料，请稍候……
t_en: Obtaining audio data, please wait...

t_ch: 能源核心的轴承坏掉了…要是不能赶紧修好的话，聚落就要变得黑咕隆咚的了。
t_en: The energy core's bearing component is broken... If we don't repair it soon, the settlement will be pitch-black.

t_ch: 提米，我要再去一趟铆钉镇，看看有没有可以回收利用的元件。我不在的时候，你要帮克拉拉看守好大门哦。
t_en: Timmy, I'm going back to Rivet Town — maybe I can recover some usable components. You need to guard the gate while I'm gone.

t_ch: 录音调取结束。继续执行克拉拉的命令。
t_en: Recording recovery complete. Resuming execution of Clara's commands.

t_ch: 又回去了？真没想到，这才隔了多久，咱们又得回去找人了。
t_en: She went back? But we were just there. *sigh* Sounds like we better head over again.

t_ch: 啊，原来你们之前是去别的小镇观光啦？
t_en: Wait, you guys went sightseeing in another town?

t_ch: …怕是没你想的那么轻松自在。
t_en: ...It wasn't as fun as you think.

t_ch: 那座镇上危机四伏…
t_en: That town has danger lurking in every corner.

t_ch: 的确，一想到那个小姑娘独自进入裂界，我就有点担心。
t_en: Indeed. And that little girl heading into the Fragmentum all by herself... It doesn't bear thinking about.

t_ch: 回去找克拉拉吧。
t_en: We should go and find Clara.

t_ch: 一想到那个小姑娘独自进入裂界，我就有点担心。
t_en: That little girl heading into the Fragmentum all by herself... It doesn't bear thinking about.
